methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $149M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Valar Atomics is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Isaiah Taylor that has raised $149M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers: three public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and eight private firms including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo. The narrow peer set—same-stage and adjacent-stage comparable companies—proved too sparse to support median-based triangulation, yielding fewer than three datapoints after filtering.

Consequently, only the stage-progression method was applicable. This approach applied a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $149M total raised as a valuation proxy, implying a post-Series A valuation of $298M. This multiplier reflects typical progression economics from Series A to subsequent institutional rounds in early-stage venture financings.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $253M (low) to $343M (median $298M), anchored entirely on stage-progression mechanics. The narrow confidence in this range stems from reliance on a single method and the absence of sufficient same-stage nuclear energy comparables. Key uncertainties include the applicability of standard venture step-up multiples to frontier nuclear technology, where capital intensity and regulatory pathway may diverge from typical venture profiles, and the lack of public-market trading multiples specific to early-stage nuclear platforms.
